title: System and method to indicate status of photovoltaic cell for battery of electrically operated window shading
abstract: Embodiments of the present disclosure provide an indicator system, and related methods for a photovoltaic cell. A system may include a circuit for electrical coupling between the photovoltaic cell and a battery for an electrically operated window shading. The system includes a voltage sensor for detecting a cell voltage generated in the photovoltaic cell. Control logic of the circuit detects whether the cell voltage exceeds a first voltage threshold corresponding to the photovoltaic cell being in an open circuit configuration, and whether the cell voltage exceeds a second voltage threshold corresponding to the photovoltaic cell is coupled to a battery having a predetermined charge loss. A set of indicators are coupled to the control logic of the circuit, and the control logic selectively enables the set of indicators in response to the cell voltage exceeding the first voltage threshold and/or the second voltage threshold.
claims: Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. An indicator system for a photovoltaic cell, the indicator system comprising: a circuit configured for electrical coupling between the photovoltaic cell and a battery for an electrically operated window shading, wherein the circuit includes: a voltage sensor for detecting a cell voltage generated in the photovoltaic cell, and control logic within the circuit, the control logic configured to detect whether the cell voltage exceeds a first voltage threshold corresponding to the photovoltaic cell being in an open circuit configuration, and whether the cell voltage exceeds a second voltage threshold corresponding to the photovoltaic cell being coupled to a battery having a predetermined charge loss; and a set of indicators coupled to the control logic of the circuit, wherein the control logic selectively enables the set of indicators in response to the cell voltage exceeding the first voltage threshold or the second voltage threshold. 2. The indicator system of claim 1, further comprising a casing for housing the circuit and the set of indicators, wherein the casing comprises a cap mounted on a lateral end of the photovoltaic cell. 3. The indicator system of claim 1, wherein the circuit and the set of indicators are embedded within a casing for housing a portion of the photovoltaic cell. 4. The indicator system of claim 1, wherein the set of indicators includes at least one light emitting diode (LED) configured to emit a plurality of colors, each of the plurality of colors corresponding to a respective level of the cell voltage. 5. The indicator system of claim 1, further comprising a current indicator configured to detect a current flow from the photovoltaic cell, wherein the control logic selectively enables the set of indicators in response to detecting the current flow. 6. The indicator system of claim 1, wherein the first voltage threshold and the second voltage threshold are based on a predetermined battery voltage sufficient to actuate the battery-operated window shading. 7. The indicator system of claim 1, wherein the second voltage threshold is based on a predetermined current sufficient to charge the battery at a rate that is above a current drain of the battery. 8. The indicator system of claim 1, wherein the set of indicators is configurable between a first state to indicate electrical operability of the battery, and a second state to indicate charging of the battery based on the detected cell voltage and the detected current. 9. The indicator system of claim 1, wherein the set of indicators includes at least one light emitting diode (LED) selectable between a plurality of illumination settings, each of the plurality of illumination settings corresponding to a respective level of the cell voltage. 10. The indicator system of claim 1, wherein the first voltage threshold is based on a voltage at which the cell voltage is sufficient to maintain a charge level of the battery at or above a minimum level for operating the motor of the battery-operated window shading. 11. A method to indicate a status of a photovoltaic cell configured for a battery-operated window shading, the method comprising: determining whether a cell voltage generated in the photovoltaic cell exceeds a first voltage threshold corresponding to the photovoltaic cell being in an open circuit configuration; indicating whether the cell voltage is one of exceeding the first voltage threshold or not exceeding the first voltage threshold; determining whether the cell voltage exceeds a second voltage threshold corresponding to the photovoltaic cell being coupled to a battery having a predetermined charge loss; and indicating whether the cell voltage is one of exceeding the second voltage threshold or not exceeding the second voltage threshold. 12. The method of claim 11, further comprising: decoupling the photovoltaic cell from the battery of the battery-operated window shading, before determining whether the cell voltage generated in the photovoltaic cell exceeds the first voltage threshold; and coupling the photovoltaic cell to the battery of the battery-operated window shading, before determining whether the cell voltage exceeds the second voltage threshold. 13. The method of claim 11, further comprising calculating the second voltage threshold based on a predetermined battery voltage sufficient to actuate the battery-operated window shading. 14. The method of claim 11, further comprising calculating the second voltage threshold based on a predetermined current sufficient to charge the battery at a rate that is above a current drain of the battery. 15. The method of claim 11, further comprising: detecting a current flow from the photovoltaic cell to the battery; and indicating whether the current flow from the photovoltaic cell to the battery is detected. 16. A system comprising: a photovoltaic cell for converting electromagnetic radiation into a cell voltage; a circuit coupled to the photovoltaic cell, and configured for coupling the photovoltaic cell to a window shading including an adjustable panel, a motor for actuating the adjustable panel, and a battery for operating the motor, wherein the circuit includes control logic configured to perform actions including: monitoring a cell voltage generated in the photovoltaic cell, comparing the cell voltage with a first voltage threshold corresponding to the photovoltaic cell being in an open circuit configuration, and comparing the cell voltage with a second voltage threshold corresponding to the photovoltaic cell being coupled to a battery having a predetermined charge loss; and a set of indicators configured to indicate whether the cell voltage exceeds the first voltage threshold, or the second voltage threshold. 17. The system of claim 16, wherein the control circuit is within a casing coupled to the photovoltaic cell. 18. The system of claim 16, wherein the set of indicators includes at least one light emitting diode (LED) configured to emit a plurality of colors, each of the plurality of colors corresponding to a respective level of the cell voltage or the current. 19. The system of claim 16, wherein the set of indicators includes a plurality of light emitting diodes (LEDs), wherein a subset of the array of LEDs corresponds to a respective level of the cell voltage or the current. 19 20. The system of claim 16, further comprising a current indicator configured to detect a current flow from the photovoltaic cell, wherein the control logic selectively enables the set of indicators in response to detecting the current flow. 21. The system of claim 16, wherein the second voltage threshold is based on a predetermined battery voltage sufficient to actuate the battery-operated window shading. 22. The system of claim 16, wherein the first voltage threshold is based on a predetermined current sufficient to charge the battery at a rate that is above a current drain of the battery. 23. The system of claim 16, wherein the control circuit and the set of indicators are embedded within a casing for housing the photovoltaic cell. 24. The system of claim 16, further comprising the window shading coupled to the circuit, the window shading including the adjustable panel, the motor for actuating the adjustable panel, and the battery for operating the motor. 25. The system of claim 16, wherein the first voltage threshold is based on a voltage at which the cell voltage is sufficient to maintain a charge level of the battery at or above a minimum level for operating the motor of the battery-operated window shading. 20
